title: Tracking holidays for employees in hours/time spent

posts.author: hbars1991 (Heather)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

We currently track employees time to complete tasks and work on an available capacity of 6 hours per day.

We want to log any holidays that people take so that it doesn’t look like they haven’t logged any work for those days but the issue comes when the holiday spans over a few weeks.

For example, if I have a holiday for Friday and Monday (we don’t work weekends) I would need 6 hours attributed to the Friday and 6 to the Monday - since they’re in different weeks, I can’t use the due date functionality as it would assign 12 hours to either the first or the second week. Also, it would be annoying to add 2 different tasks for the different days.

Has anyone had similar experiences? Any workarounds?

TIA

posts.author: sirah (Sirah E)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @hbars1991 sirah here from Mindflows

Can I ask what plan you are on? For Pro and Enterprise Plan the Time Tracking Column can be a huge help here! More information on the time tracking column on this link: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360001143809-The-Time-Tracking-Column

This also includes manual input and better transparency for each task. I hope this helps!

posts.author: Clarey (MondayMotivator)
posts.cookedHtml:
Heather:

Hi there,

We currently track employees time to complete tasks and work on an available capacity of 6 hours per day.

We want to log any holidays that people take so that it doesn’t look like they haven’t logged any work for those days but the issue comes when the holiday spans over a few weeks.

For example, if I have a holiday for Friday and Monday (we don’t work weekends) I would need 6 hours attributed to the Friday and 6 to the Monday - since they’re in different weeks, I can’t use the due date functionality as it would assign 12 hours to either the first or the second week. Also, it would be annoying to add 2 different tasks for the different days.

Has anyone had similar experiences? Any workarounds?

Hi there,

We’ve run into a similar situation before, and while Monday.com doesn’t yet support multi-day capacity allocation within a single item natively, there are a few workarounds that might help:

Option 1: Use Subitems for Each Day

You can create a main task called “Holiday” and then use subitems to represent each day off (e.g., “Friday - Holiday,” “Monday - Holiday”). This allows you to assign exactly 6 hours per subitem and ensures the time is logged accurately across different weeks.

Option 2: Use Time Tracking with Manual Entry

If you’re using the Time Tracking column, you can manually log 6 hours on Friday and 6 on Monday—even for the same task. This way, the data will reflect the right distribution across days without needing separate tasks. Let me know with a like if this helps or feel free to ask for more ideas / help!

posts.author: Michaleiba (Michal Leiba)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, I am Michal from the spot-nik team. We just launched a new app- SuperPlanner. I believe it answers your needs. I wonder if this is still relevant for you, but here it is-

https://auth.monday.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=a72397016954aa33e272936988566142&response_type=install

Let me know what you think
